guidelines.title: Important Guidelines

guidelines.auditDesc: Your IP address, user agent, and timestamp will be logged for transparency.

guides.title: Internal Guides
guides.subtitle: System reference documentation for the team. All content is based on actual system logic.
guides.sensitivityDesc: Complete reference for all 15 allergen types, their keywords, matching logic, exclusion rules, and how the detection system classifies them.
guides.votingDesc: How points are earned and deducted, the weighted voting system, trust ranks, abuse detection, and how votes affect product verification.
guides.step1Normalize: Normalize — Remove diacritics, normalize Arabic text
guides.step2Tokenize: Tokenize — Split ingredients into word tokens
guides.step3Exclude: Exclude Check — Filter known false positives (buckwheat, nutmeg, coconut, etc.)
guides.step4Exact: Exact Match — Compare normalized allergen name to ingredient
guides.step5Token: Token Match — Word-boundary matching (plural forms, compound words, Arabic article)
guides.step6Taxonomy: Taxonomy Match — Keyword-based matching against the allergen taxonomy
guides.principle1: The system never uses the word "safe" — uses "NOT_DETECTED" instead
guides.principle3: Detection priority order ensures compound words like "peanut butter" are attributed correctly
guides.principle4: Wheat allergy and Gluten intolerance are treated as separate conditions
guides.principle5: E322 (lecithin) is NOT automatically mapped to soy — only explicit "soy lecithin" triggers
guides.pointsSystem: Points System — How Points Are Earned & Deducted
guides.contributionCreated: User submits a product contribution (new product, edit ingredients, add image, report error)
guides.contributionApproved: Bonus when admin approves a contribution
guides.experienceCreated: User shares an allergic reaction review or general experience
guides.experienceVote: User votes "helpful" on another user's experience
guides.adminAdjustment: Manual adjustment by admin (can be positive or negative)
guides.bonusReward: Special rewards (leaderboard bonus, achievements, community verification bonus +5)
guides.experienceDeleted: User deletes their own experience (refunds original award)
guides.pointsFloor: Points can never go below 0 (enforced in code).
guides.badgeUnlock: Badges are unlocked automatically when a user reaches the badge's required points threshold
guides.badgeOnce: Each badge can only be earned once (immutable)
guides.badgeCategories: Badge categories: contribution, accuracy, social, general
guides.flow1: User submits a new product contribution →
guides.productFieldVoting: Product Field Voting — Weighted Trust System
guides.votingIntro: The trust voting system is separate from the points system. Users vote on product data fields to verify accuracy. Votes are weighted based on the voter's rank and accuracy history.
guides.approveDesc: Confirms the field data is correct
guides.flagDesc: Marks for admin review (may require legal consideration)
guides.dailyCap: Daily cap: 30 votes per user per day
guides.monthlyCap: Monthly cap: 500 votes per user per month
guides.timingAbuse: Timing: Votes faster than 2 seconds apart trigger abuse detection
guides.timeDecayDesc: Older votes lose influence over time:
guides.timeDecayFloor: Minimum weight floor: 0.1 — votes never fully disappear.
guides.explorerDesc: Starting rank for all new users
guides.verifierDesc: Trusted community member with proven track record
guides.guardianDesc: Highly experienced verifier with strong accuracy
guides.expertDesc: Domain expert assigned by admin (e.g., nutritionist, food scientist)
guides.adminDesc: Full override capability with mandatory documentation
guides.accuracyDesc: Accuracy directly multiplies vote weight. A user with 50% accuracy has half the effective weight of a user with 100% accuracy at the same rank.
guides.productVerification: Product Verification — How Votes Determine Status
guides.communityVerifiedCond: Trust score ≥ 20, no active contest, not frozen
guides.expertVerifiedCond: Trust score ≥ 20, expert or admin has voted and verified
guides.underReviewCond: Field frozen by admin for manual investigation
guides.allergenVerifier: At least 1 vote from Verifier rank or higher
guides.allergenScore25: Minimum trust score: 25 (vs 20 for other fields)
guides.allergenStricterNote: This ensures allergen data is verified by experienced users before being marked as trusted.
guides.approvalStep1: User submits a product contribution → status: community_review
guides.approvalStep3: 3 approve votes → auto-creates product, marks contribution approved, contributor gets +5 bonus
guides.approvalStep5: When ALL core fields (allergens, ingredients_ar, overall) reach community_verified → product marked as verified: true
guides.approvalStep6: Verified products rank higher in search results
guides.adminVoteDocs: Mandatory documentation — reason field cannot be null
guides.adminVoteAudit: Full audit trail: IP address, user agent, timestamp
guides.adminVoteLegal: Optional legal review flag for compliance-sensitive decisions
guides.adminVotePublic: All admin votes are visible in the public audit trail — no silent overrides
guides.freezeDesc1: Admin can freeze any product field to prevent further voting
guides.freezeDesc4: Only admin role can freeze fields
guides.pointsAdjustDesc: Admins can manually adjust any user's points (positive or negative) via the Gamification page. All adjustments are logged as ADMIN_ADJUSTMENT transactions.
guides.abuseIntro: The system automatically monitors voting patterns and flags suspicious activity. All detections are logged and can trigger automatic actions.
guides.coordinatedVotingRule: Multiple accounts voting same pattern (85%+ similarity)
guides.flaggedWeightReduction: Flagged users have their vote weight reduced by 50%.
guides.experienceVotingIntro: This is a simpler system separate from product field voting. Users can mark community experiences as helpful or unhelpful.
guides.expVote1: Each helpful vote increments the experience's counter by 1
guides.expVote3: No weight system — all votes count equally
guides.expVote4: The voter earns 2 points for casting a helpful vote
guides.expVote5: Experiences are ranked by helpful vote count (higher = more visible)
guides.audit1: Trust event log is append-only (immutable). Every vote, score change, status transition, and admin action is recorded.
guides.audit2: Points transactions are append-only. Every point award/deduction is a separate record.
guides.audit3: Admin votes have full metadata: IP address, user agent, mandatory reason, optional legal review flag.
guides.audit4: Vote snapshots: Each vote stores the voter's trust score and rank at the time of voting (immutable snapshot).
guides.audit5: Optimistic locking on product field trust records prevents race conditions in concurrent voting.
guides.allergen_milk_desc: Covers all dairy-derived proteins and derivatives. Includes lactose, casein, whey, and all milk-based products. One of the most common food allergies, especially in children.
guides.allergen_milk_handles: Word-boundary matching ensures "milk" in "milk chocolate" is detected but avoids false matches. All dairy derivatives trigger this allergen type.
guides.allergen_egg_desc: Covers egg proteins from both white (albumin) and yolk (vitellin). Also includes E1105 (lysozyme from egg white) and derivatives like meringue.
guides.allergen_egg_handles: E1105 is a mapped E-number that directly triggers egg detection. "egg" as a token matches via word-boundary matching.
guides.allergen_fish_desc: Covers all fish species and fish-derived products. Cross-reactive with the seafood group (shellfish).
guides.allergen_fish_handles: Each fish species name is a separate keyword. Token matching catches compound names like "fish sauce" or "fish oil".
guides.allergen_shellfish_crustacean_desc: Covers crustacean species: shrimp, crab, lobster, crayfish. Separate from molluscs (EU distinction).
guides.allergen_shellfish_crustacean_handles: Matched separately from molluscs. Users allergic to crustaceans may not be allergic to molluscs and vice versa.
guides.allergen_shellfish_mollusc_desc: EU-required: covers mollusc species (oyster, mussel, squid, octopus). Separated from crustaceans per EU 1169/2011.
guides.allergen_shellfish_mollusc_handles: Separate allergen type from crustacean shellfish. Both fall under the seafood cross-reactivity group.
guides.allergen_tree_nuts_desc: Covers all tree nut species. Has critical exclude keywords to prevent false positives from "nutmeg" (a seed spice) and "coconut" (a drupe).
guides.allergen_tree_nuts_handles: Exclude keywords prevent "nutmeg" (seed spice), "coconut" (drupe), and "butternut" (squash) from triggering tree nut detection. Arabic normalization converts ة→ه so "جوزة الطيب" also matches the exclude list.
guides.allergen_peanuts_desc: Peanuts are legumes, not tree nuts. Cross-reactive with soy and lupin (legume group). Detection priority is set higher than milk to ensure "peanut butter" is attributed to peanuts, not milk.
guides.allergen_peanuts_handles: Peanuts has the HIGHEST detection priority. When "peanut butter" is encountered, it matches peanuts first (not milk from "butter"). This is enforced by the priority order in ALLERGEN_TAXONOMY.
guides.allergen_wheat_desc: Covers wheat species and derivatives. Medically distinct from gluten intolerance (IgE-mediated vs. autoimmune). Has extensive exclude keywords to prevent false positives from buckwheat (a pseudocereal) and safe alternative flours.
guides.allergen_wheat_handles: Extensive exclusion list prevents false positives. "buckwheat" and "black wheat flour" (SFDA translation of buckwheat) are excluded because buckwheat is a pseudocereal (Fagopyrum), NOT wheat. Safe flours (rice, corn, tapioca, potato, chickpea) are excluded. "gluten free" label claims are excluded.
guides.allergen_gluten_desc: Covers gluten-containing grains: wheat, barley, rye, and oats (per EU 1169/2011). Medically distinct from wheat allergy — gluten intolerance/celiac is autoimmune, not IgE-mediated. Oats are included because they commonly cause issues for celiac patients unless certified gluten-free.
guides.allergen_gluten_handles: User with Wheat allergy → only wheat keywords checked, barley/rye NOT flagged. User with Gluten allergy → barley, rye, malt, oats all flagged. "Gluten free oats" are excluded because certified GF oats are safe for most celiac patients.
guides.allergen_soy_desc: Covers soy/soya and derivatives. Cross-reactive with peanuts and lupin (legume group). E322 (lecithin) is NOT automatically mapped to soy — only "soy lecithin" triggers, since lecithin can be derived from sunflower or egg.
guides.allergen_soy_handles: E322 is intentionally NOT mapped to soy because lecithin can be derived from sunflower, rapeseed, or egg. Only when the ingredient explicitly says "soy lecithin" does it trigger soy detection.
guides.allergen_sesame_desc: Added to FDA major allergens in 2023 (FASTER Act). Covers sesame seeds, oil, and derived products like tahini and halva.
guides.allergen_sesame_handles: Multiple regional names are covered. Halva is included because it is primarily sesame-based in GCC/Middle Eastern products.
guides.allergen_celery_desc: EU-required allergen. Less common in GCC market but included for EU compliance. Covers celery root (celeriac), seeds, and salt.
guides.allergen_celery_handles: Standard keyword matching. Low severity classification.
guides.allergen_mustard_desc: EU-required allergen. Covers mustard seeds, powder, oil, and prepared mustard products.
guides.allergen_mustard_handles: Standard keyword matching. "Dijon" specifically added as a mustard variety.
guides.allergen_lupin_desc: EU-required allergen. Cross-reactive with peanuts (legume group). Lupin flour is increasingly used in gluten-free products as a wheat flour substitute.
guides.allergen_lupin_handles: Part of the legume cross-reactivity group (with peanuts and soy). Users with peanut allergy should be aware of potential cross-reactivity.
guides.allergen_sulfites_desc: Preservative compounds (E220-E228). Required labeling when concentration exceeds 10 mg/kg. Common in dried fruits, wine, and processed foods.
guides.allergen_sulfites_handles: Full E-number range E220-E228 mapped. Both American (sulfite) and British (sulphite) spelling variants covered.
